LAW AND MOTION, 302, AS TO DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE AND TAYLOR SMITH, M.D. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION AS UNOPPOSED. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH HER PRIMA FACIE CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE AND TAYLOR SMITH, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE IS NOT TIME-BARRED. (SEE CAL. CODE CIV. PROC., 335.1; NORGART V. UPJOHN CO. (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 383.) PLAINTIFF HAS PROPERLY PLED THAT DEFENDANTS FAILED TO PROVIDE MEDICAL CARE WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS IN DEFENDANTS' CARE AND CUSTODY. (SEE, COMPLAINT, 9-10.) PLAINTIFF NEED NOT PLEAD INABILITY TO CARE FOR HER OWN BASIC NEEDS TO ESTABLISH A CLAIM FOR ELDER ABUSE. (SEE, WELF. & INST. CODE, 15600 ET SEQ.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THIS DEFENDANT. SEE CCP 430.10(E). PLAINTIFF LUMPS ALL OF THE DEFENDANTS TOGETHER AND DOES NOT ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS REGARDING DEFENDANT HOSPITAL'S WRONGDOING. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE 'NEGLECT' WITHIN THE MEANING OF WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE SEC. 15610.57. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE MISCONDUCT DISTINCT FROM PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE. THE ALLEGATIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT HOSPITAL DO NOT SHOW A 'FAILURE OF THOSE RESPONSIBLE FOR ATTENDING TO THE BASIC NEEDS AND COMFORTS OF ELDERLY OR DEPENDENT ADULTS' OR A 'FAILURE TO PROVIDE MEDICAL CARE.' COVENANT CARE, INC. V. SUP. CRT. (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 771, 783 (EMPHASIS IN ORIGINAL). THE DEMURRER TO THE BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF LUMPS ALL OF THE DEFENDANTS TOGETHER. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS AGAINST THE HOSPITAL (AS OPPOSED TO THE TREATING PHYSICIAN) SHOWING HOW DEFENDANT HOSPITAL DELIBERATELY DEVIATED FROM THE PATIENT'S CONSENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COURT WILL REVIEW PROPOSED JUDGMENT SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE AND TAYLOR SMITH, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, BECAUSE CCP 425.13 APPLIES TO CLAIMS FOR BATTERY THAT ARISE FROM THE RENDITION OF MEDICAL SERVICES. (SEE, CENTRAL PATHOLOGY SERVICE MEDICAL CLINIC, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 3 CAL.4TH 181, 192.) THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE "MEDICAL BATTERY" LANGUAGE IS DENIED, AS PLAINTIFF HAS PROPERLY STATED A CLAIM FOR MEDICAL BATTERY. (SEE, SAXENA V. GOFFNEY (2008) 159 CAL.APP.4TH 316, 324; COMPLAINT 28-30.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)

JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar May-04-2015 Continued To May-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 502

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT V. GOOD of The Goldman Law Firm, Tel No. (415) 435-5500 appearing for Defendants California Pacific Orthopaedic and Sports Medicine and Taylor Smith, M.D.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE AND TAYLOR SMITH, M.D. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, the court grants summary adjudication on the battery cause of action as unopposed. The court grants summary adjudication on the Elder Abuse cause of action because plaintiff cannot establish her prima facie claim. "[N]eglect within the meaning of Welfare and Institutions Code section 15610.57 covers an area of misconduct distinct from professional negligence. As used in the Act, neglect refers not to the substandard performance of medical services but, rather, to the failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs and comforts of elderly or dependent adults, regardless of their professional standing, to carry out their custodial obligations. Thus, the statutory definition of neglect speaks not of the undertaking of medical services, but of the failure to provide medical care." (Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 783 [original italics, internal citations omitted]; see also Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, 31; Worsham v. O'Connor Hospital (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 331.) In this case, defendants' facts are largely unopposed and show that defendants were providing medical care to plaintiff and did not deprive plaintiff of custodial services necessary to her well being. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2015 Continued To Apr-17-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Continued To Mar-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SUSIE INJIJIAN, ESQ., 510.898.1478, FOR PLAINTIFF YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS.
CORINNA MEISSNER, ESQ., 415.288.9800, FOR Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals.
ROBERT GOOD, ESQ., 415.435.5500, FOR DEFENDANTS TAYLOR SMITH MD AND CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' Demurrer To Complaint. The demurrer to the cause of action for elder abuse is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff fails to state a cause of action against this defendant. See CCP 430.10(e). Plaintiff lumps all of the defendants together and does not allege specific facts regarding defendant hospital's wrongdoing. Plaintiff does not allege 'neglect' within the meaning of Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 15610.57. Plaintiff does not allege misconduct distinct from professional negligence. The allegations against defendant hospital do not show a 'failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs and comforts of elderly or dependent adults' or a 'failure to provide medical care.' Covenant Care, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 783 (emphasis in original).
The demurrer to the battery cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff lumps all of the defendants together. Plaintiff does not allege specific facts against the hospital (as opposed to the treating physician) showing how defendant hospital deliberately deviated from the patient's consent. Order signed in open court. Court will review proposed judgment submitted by counsel. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SUSIE INJIJIAN, ESQ., 510.898.1478, FOR PLAINTIFF YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS.
CORINNA MEISSNER, ESQ., 415.288.9800, FOR Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals.
ROBERT GOOD, ESQ., 415.435.5500, FOR DEFENDANTS TAYLOR SMITH MD AND CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' Motion To Strike Complaint is granted in its entirety. The Motion is granted with respect to the prayer for punitive damages because CCP 425.13 applies to claims for battery that arise from the rendition of medical services. See Central Pathology Service Medical Clinic, Inc. v. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181, 192.) The Motion to Strike is granted as to the claim for fees. Plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts to support the award under Welfare & Institutions Code sec. 15657. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SUSIE INIJIAN, TEL. NO. (510) 898-1478 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE and TAYLOR SMITH, M.D.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. The cause of action for Elder Abuse is not time-barred. (See Cal. Code Civ. Proc., 335.1; Norgart v. Upjohn Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 383.) Plaintiff has properly pled that Defendants failed to provide medical care while Plaintiff was in Defendants' care and custody. (See, Complaint, 9-10.) Plaintiff need not plead inability to care for her own basic needs to establish a claim for Elder Abuse. (See, Welf. & Inst. Code, 15600 et seq.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SUSIE INIJIAN, TEL. NO. (510) 898-1478 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE and TAYLOR SMITH, M.D.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted in part and overruled in part. The Motion is granted with respect to the prayer for punitive damages, because CCP 425.13 applies to claims for battery that arise from the rendition of medical services. (See, Central Pathology Service Medical Clinic, Inc. v. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181, 192.) The Motion to Strike the "Medical Battery" language is denied, as Plaintiff has properly stated a claim for Medical Battery. (See, Saxena v. Goffney (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 316, 324; Complaint 28-30.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
